title: Two continuity validators enforce different relation-integrity guarantees
summary: At the original observation, the aggregate validator and the separate relation validator enforced partially overlapping contracts. The repository-wide validator was later composed with strict relation integrity, so this fragmentation finding is now superseded by CM-1001. The original contradiction wording remains preserved through the prior blob SHA and the linked target record CM-1002 rather than being left as prose inside a machine-relation array.

legacy_relation_narrative.original_value: Any current-state claim that one repository-wide continuity validator demonstrably enforces every relation guarantee implemented by both validator modules

status_relations.observed: The two modules originally had overlapping but non-identical acceptance rules and different input interfaces.
status_relations.inferred: Before composition, a record set could satisfy the aggregate validator while still containing a duplicate relation target or supersession cycle rejected by the separate relation validator.
status_relations.proposed: Keep one aggregate validator, one adversarial suite, and ID-only machine relations as the authoritative continuity contract.
status_relations.superseded: The prior fragmented-validator state and the prose-only contradiction edge have both been replaced by composed validation and a linked record.
status_relations.unresolved: No completed CI run at the exact migration commit has yet been observed.

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch the original CM-1000 blob 131fa02930e7fd2314aaea6f1e649e1f7022af0a, the current aggregate validator, CM-1001, and CM-1002. Falsify the historical claim by showing the two original validators had identical accepted domains or were already composed. Falsify the migration claim by showing the original prose is not preserved or the current relation target does not resolve. Structural validity does not establish truth of indexed claims.
